components.name: User Interface
components.description: Manages the graphical user interface for the calculator application. Responsible for displaying the main application window, rendering the numeric display area, and managing all interactive UI elements including digit buttons (0-9), arithmetic operator buttons (+, -, ×, ÷), a clear button, and an equals button. Handles user input events from the UI and updates the display accordingly. Acts as the presentation layer, translating user interactions into commands for the calculation engine.

components.name: Calculation Engine
components.description: Implements the core mathematical logic of the calculator application. Responsible for performing arithmetic operations (addition, subtraction, multiplication, division) with proper input validation. Handles expression parsing, evaluation, and result computation. Includes error handling mechanisms to recover from invalid operations such as division by zero or malformed input syntax. Provides immediate result feedback to the user interface layer. This component encapsulates all business logic related to numerical computation and ensures mathematical correctness.

predicates.name: aggregates
predicates.description: Whole-part relationship where the part can exist independently

predicates.name: composes
predicates.description: Strong whole-part relationship where the part is owned by the whole

predicates.name: realizes
predicates.description: A class implements/realizes an interface or contract

predicates.name: invokes
predicates.description: Weak association, signififying a caller-callee relationship

high_level_requirements.description: The application displays a GUI window with a numeric display area and buttons for digits 0-9, basic arithmetic operators (+, -, ×, ÷), a clear button, and an equals button

The calculator performs addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division operations with proper input validation, returns results immediately, and recovers from errors such as division by zero or invalid syntax

low_level_requirements.description: The application launches and renders a main GUI window.
The GUI window contains a numeric display area for showing input and results.
The GUI window contains buttons for digits 0 through 9.
The GUI window contains buttons for basic arithmetic operators: addition (+), subtraction (-), multiplication (×), and division (÷).
The GUI window contains a clear button.
The GUI window contains an equals button.
The engine calculates the sum of two valid numeric operands.
The engine calculates the difference of two valid numeric operands.
The engine calculates the product of two valid numeric operands.
The engine calculates the quotient of two valid numeric operands where the divisor is not zero.
The engine rejects inputs that are not valid numbers or contain invalid syntax.
The engine handles division by zero by raising an appropriate error instead of crashing.
The engine returns the result immediately upon receiving a valid operation request.

ontology_nodes.name: Operation
ontology_nodes.description: An enumeration of supported arithmetic operations: ADD, SUBTRACT, MULTIPLY, and DIVIDE.

ontology_nodes.name: CalculatorEngine
ontology_nodes.description: The core calculator engine class that performs arithmetic operations (addition, subtraction, multiplication, division) with input validation and error handling.

ontology_nodes.name: precision
ontology_nodes.description: The number of decimal places for result precision.

ontology_nodes.name: add
ontology_nodes.description: Calculates the sum of two valid numeric operands.

ontology_nodes.name: subtract
ontology_nodes.description: Calculates the difference of two valid numeric operands.

ontology_nodes.name: multiply
ontology_nodes.description: Calculates the product of two valid numeric operands.

ontology_nodes.name: divide
ontology_nodes.description: Calculates the quotient of two valid numeric operands where the divisor is not zero.

ontology_nodes.name: validateInput
ontology_nodes.description: Validates that the inputs are valid numbers and rejects invalid syntax.

ontology_nodes.name: CalculatorResult
ontology_nodes.description: A result wrapper class that holds the outcome of a calculator operation, including success/failure status, the computed value, and error messages.

ontology_nodes.name: value
ontology_nodes.description: The computed numeric result.

ontology_nodes.name: is_success
ontology_nodes.description: Indicates whether the operation succeeded.

ontology_nodes.name: error_message
ontology_nodes.description: Error message if the operation failed.

ontology_nodes.name: get_value
ontology_nodes.description: Returns the computed value.

ontology_nodes.name: is_error
ontology_nodes.description: Returns true if the operation resulted in an error.

ontology_nodes.name: get_error
ontology_nodes.description: Returns the error message if the operation failed.

ontology_nodes.name: CalculatorWindow
ontology_nodes.description: The main application window for the calculator. Extends Fl_Double_Window to provide a flicker-free GUI with a numeric display and all required buttons.

ontology_nodes.name: display
ontology_nodes.description: The numeric display area showing input and results

ontology_nodes.name: clearButton
ontology_nodes.description: The clear button to reset the calculator state

ontology_nodes.name: equalsButton
ontology_nodes.description: The equals button that triggers calculation on click or Enter key

ontology_nodes.name: digitButtons
ontology_nodes.description: Buttons for digits 0 through 9

ontology_nodes.name: operatorButtons
ontology_nodes.description: Buttons for arithmetic operators: +, -, ×, ÷

ontology_nodes.name: currentInput
ontology_nodes.description: The current input string being built by the user

ontology_nodes.name: lastResult
ontology_nodes.description: The last computed result to display

ontology_nodes.name: needsNewInput
ontology_nodes.description: Flag indicating whether the next digit starts a new input (after equals or clear)

ontology_nodes.name: CalculatorWindow
ontology_nodes.description: Constructor that initializes the window, display, and all buttons with their callbacks.

ontology_nodes.name: ~CalculatorWindow
ontology_nodes.description: Destructor for cleanup.

ontology_nodes.name: initializeDisplay
ontology_nodes.description: Creates and configures the Fl_Output display widget.

ontology_nodes.name: initializeDigitButtons
ontology_nodes.description: Creates and configures the digit buttons (0-9) with their callbacks.

ontology_nodes.name: initializeOperatorButtons
ontology_nodes.description: Creates and configures the operator buttons (+, -, ×, ÷) with their callbacks.

ontology_nodes.name: initializeClearButton
ontology_nodes.description: Creates and configures the clear button with its callback.

ontology_nodes.name: initializeEqualsButton
ontology_nodes.description: Creates and configures the equals button with its callback.

ontology_nodes.name: handleDigitInput
ontology_nodes.description: Callback for digit button clicks. Appends the digit to the current input and updates the display.

ontology_nodes.name: handleOperatorInput
ontology_nodes.description: Callback for operator button clicks. Stores the operator and prepares for the next operand.

ontology_nodes.name: handleClear
ontology_nodes.description: Callback for the clear button. Resets the calculator state and clears the display.

ontology_nodes.name: handleEquals
ontology_nodes.description: Callback for the equals button. Triggers calculation and displays the result.

ontology_nodes.name: updateDisplay
ontology_nodes.description: Updates the Fl_Output widget with the current input or result string.

ontology_nodes.name: performCalculation
ontology_nodes.description: Invokes the calculation engine to compute the result based on the stored operands and operator.

ontology_nodes.name: Fl_Double_Window
ontology_nodes.description: External dependency: Fl_Double_Window

ontology_nodes.name: Fl_Output
ontology_nodes.description: External dependency: Fl_Output

ontology_nodes.name: Fl_Button
ontology_nodes.description: External dependency: Fl_Button

ontology_nodes.name: Fl_Return_Button
ontology_nodes.description: External dependency: Fl_Return_Button

verification_methods.description: Verify that the application starts and a main window frame is created by the GUI toolkit.
Verify that a dedicated UI element (e.g., Label, Textarea, Display) is present in the window layout intended for numeric output.
Verify that ten distinct button elements corresponding to digits '0' through '9' are rendered in the interface.
Verify that four distinct button elements corresponding to operators '+', '-', '×', and '÷' are rendered in the interface.
Verify that a button element labeled 'Clear' (or similar standard abbreviation like 'C') is rendered in the interface.
Verify that a button element labeled 'Equals' (or '=' symbol) is rendered in the interface.
Verify that adding two valid numbers returns their correct sum.
Verify that subtracting the second operand from the first returns the correct difference.
Verify that multiplying two valid numbers returns their correct product.
Verify that dividing the first operand by the second returns the correct quotient.
Verify that providing an invalid syntax string raises a syntax error exception.
Verify that dividing by zero raises a division by zero error.
Verify that the calculation method returns a result within a defined time threshold (e.g., < 100ms).

verification_actions.description: Instantiate the CalculatorWindow object
Verify the Fl_Output display widget is created and not null
Verify the digit button vector is initialized and not empty
Verify the operator button vector is initialized and not empty
Verify the clear button is created and not null
Verify the equals button is created and not null
Inspect the CalculatorWindow class definition to locate the display member variable
Verify that the display member variable is of type Fl_Output*
Review the initializeDisplay() method to confirm it creates and configures an Fl_Output widget
Inspect the digitButtons vector of the CalculatorWindow instance
Inspect the operatorButtons vector to confirm it contains exactly four Fl_Button pointers
Verify that the first button is associated with the '+' operation
Verify that the second button is associated with the '-' operation
Verify that the third button is associated with the '×' operation
Verify that the fourth button is associated with the '÷' operation
Inspect the CalculatorWindow instance to verify the clearButton member is initialized.
Verify the clearButton widget exists and is visible in the window layout.
Inspect the CalculatorWindow instance to verify the equalsButton member is initialized.
Create a new CalculatorEngine instance
Call the add method with operands 5.5 and 2.3
Verify the result is successful
Retrieve the computed value from the result
Call the subtract method with operands 10.0 and 3.0
Check if the result is a success
Retrieve the computed value from the result
Call the multiply method with operands 3.5 and 2.0
Check that the result is successful
Get the computed value from the result
Invoke the divide method with operands 10.0 and 2.0
Capture the result of the division operation
Retrieve the computed value from the result
Create a CalculatorEngine instance with default precision
Call validateInput with an invalid syntax string like "12abc"
Verify that validateInput returns false for invalid syntax
Create a CalculatorEngine instance
Call the divide method with 10.0 as dividend and 0.0 as divisor
Invoke the add method with two valid operands
